channel.title: Media Discussion (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Media Discussion" - TV, Movies, Books and other noninteractive media, including creepypasta!

item.title: A REALLY underrated podcast: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Moved to Media Discussion.

Also, if you want people to be interested in looking at the podcast, you should mention what the subject matter/premise of it is rather than just saying "it's got good discussion" (which most people would probably assume is a minimum requirement).

item.title: A REALLY underrated podcast: A REALLY underrated podcast
item.content:encoded:
I found this podcast not that long ago but the videos in the podcast have really good discussions in them and really good editing. Yes the podcast/channel IS new but it still bothers me so go take a look at it if you don't like it leave if ya like it sub and show the support this man needs
Here's the link to the channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC3-tiMJw4PmyfQR-PlrGvZg

item.title: Favorite horror movies: Re: Favorite horror movies
item.content:encoded: My favorite horror films are The Shining, The Thing, The Exorcist, Hereditary, original Dawn of the Dead.

item.title: Favorite horror movies: Favorite horror movies
item.content:encoded:
What are your favorite horror movies?

My favorite is of course The Shining, though I also enjoy Halloween (1978). A Cure for Wellness had a lot of potential, but it fell apart in the second half.

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded:
Buff Barnie 🌚

Feast your eyes with the buff Barney!
He's to THICC!

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded: That's something else. 🌚

item.title: Favorite Music?: Re: Favorite Music?
item.content:encoded: I'm a little late, but lately I've had Poor Grammar by Roar on loop at all times! Something about it just makes my brain wiggle, haha

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded: I love the idea that God got embarassed by all his old "cool" OCs like helicoprion so killed most and put the remainder in Australia and the midnight zone.

item.title: Similarities between the universe of The Foundation and .....: Re: Similarities between the universe of The Foundation and .....
item.content:encoded:
The Librarian franchise of TV movies and the TV series. There's an extradimensional library filled with anomalous objects and in season one of the TV series their nemesis is the Serpent Brotherhood. Either The Serpent's Hand and the Wandering Library are based on that show or the creator of the show is a fan or possibly even a member of this website.

Sanctuary was a series on Syfy about a 300 year old immortal who collected abnormal creatures to protect them from the outside world. Her butler was a sasquatch. Really, you could just start assigning SCP numbers…

Jackie Chan Adventures, he worked for a secret organization that collected talismans and masks tied to supernatural entities.

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded: Indeed, a memetic agent that hints at bringing back something lost could refer to almost anything, like some mad forgotten God for instance, this is some SCP fuel right there.

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded:
Quoth wikipedia:

"A similar S was derived from the symbols of an ancient Dacian solar cult. It was called “Carligul Ciobanului”, meaning “Shepherd’s Hook” and represented bringing back something that was lost"

Well that's creepy as hell.

item.title: So Scarlet King is literally Aku from Samurai Jack?: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Moved to Media Discussion, since the conversation requires knowledge of Samurai Jack to participate in.

item.title: So Scarlet King is literally Aku from Samurai Jack?: So Scarlet King is literally Aku from Samurai Jack?
item.content:encoded:
Shapeshifting master of darkness, wants to unleash an unspeakable evil. Also, they both are horn-y. (But King is specifically horny.)
They both have daughters who are capable of destroying him, one betrays him.
So is 999 going to become a samurai wielding a magical sword of magic of love, hope and compassion?

item.title: Favorite Music?: Favorite Music?
item.content:encoded: [Post Body]

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded: Hart Island. An uninhabited island near NYC that's been home to a barracks, a POW camp, a mental asylum, a sanitarium, a homeless shelter, a reformatory school, a rehab center, and a prison, now used as a mass grave for unclaimed and unidentified remains, miscarriages, and stillbirths. The burials here are carried out by inmates, and the bodies are frequently mixed up and put in the wrong plots. Past a certain point, no more records exist of who's buried where. It's borderline impossible to visit, since it's still part of the Department of Corrections, and the waters around it are treacherous and full of rocks. Nothing inherently anomalous about any of that, but the negative energy around this place must be insane.

item.title: Flashpoint Just league Dark: Re: Flashpoint Just league Dark
item.content:encoded:
It's up to interpretation but since DC is a multiverse, I'd think it's an alternative reality, not a reset (sorry). We may think it's a reset when really, Barry has simply jumped from one reality to another.

Compare it to endgame. In that, the Avengers travel to the past to take the infinity stones to the future. In doing so, they cause alterations to the past but the future remains unchanged. This is because, at the moment they go back in time, to preserve the flow of time, the universe creates an alternative universe. This is to prevent a paradox. You're no longer altering the past because it's no longer your past. The same can be said for JLD.

In Flashpoint, barry travels back and creates an alternative reality in which his mother survives and a whole loud of s*** goes down. He then does this again and supposedly resets the universe back to what it was. He hasn't. He's now created a third alternative universe which is seemingly identical to the first. In JLD, he's now doing it a fourth time.

Unfortunately, if you want to avoid paradoxes and have as much logic in time travel, it's got to be alternative worlds. Reset just isn't possible.

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded:
Sharks seem like very streamlined, simplistic creatures. No swim bladder, have to keep swimming for their gills to work, jury-rigging their skin to make teeth, etc. They don't even have bones! Just a swimming digestive system with a bunch of sensory organs in front. What's up with that?

No wonder the SPC wants to punch them so bad.

item.title: Is there any websites similar to the SCP Wiki?: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Moved to Media Discussion.

item.title: Is there any websites similar to the SCP Wiki?: Re: Is there any websites similar to the SCP Wiki?
item.content:encoded:
The Wayward Society.

The Wayward Society is a volunteer organization dedicated to the eradication and investigation of dangerous, paranormal, and otherwise anomalous entities. Here, you will find documentation on the findings of those who have risked their lives or sacrificed themselves for this goal.

Its creed and mission is kinda similar to our Wiki, but unlike others it's not directly derived from SCPF (at least inspired by it, say).

Personal perspective: WwdS is more "classical" and gives an undertone of early-modern mystical, uncanny fantasies and while Foundation relies more on modern sci & tech and resembles hardcore sci-fi.

item.title: It's An Anomalous World: Re: It's An Anomalous World
item.content:encoded:
A shark born without skin or teeth was recently caught by a fishing trawler off the coast of Sardinia. Though dead by the time it was discovered in the trawler's haul, has been estimated to be about 3 years old, and an otherwise healthy example of the blackmouth catshark species.

It is currently believed that its condition was simply a very weird genetic mutation, but researchers are looking into possible pollution factors as well, whether as a congenital cause or as a cause for the mutation.

The reason for the lack of teeth ties into some interesting secondary real-world weirdness: the teeth of sharks and their evolutionary siblings are essentially really big scales that use some of the standard procedural programming for chordate teeth They grow from the gums rather than the jaw- ergo, no gums, no teeth. When you see those megalodon jaws with teeth, the jaw is usually more like a really fancy display case: even in the rare cases where a preserved jaw is found with it's original teeth, there's nothing to hold them in place except some grooves.
